steps.title: Use the cube volume formula
steps.content: A cube's volume is the cube of its edge length: $V=s^3$. With edge length $42$, compute $42^3$.

steps.title: Apply the cube volume formula
steps.content: The volume of a cube equals the cube of its edge length. With edge 42 inches, we compute $V = 42^3$.

steps.title: Calculate 42 squared
steps.content:
First, compute $42 \times 42$:
$$42 \times 42 = 1,764$$
Note that 1,764 is the remaining alternative — a common error for students who use area instead of volume.

steps.title: Multiply by 42 once more
steps.content:
Now we compute $1,764 \times 42$ by splitting into tens and ones:
$$1,764 \times 40 = 70,560$$
$$1,764 \times 2 = 3,528$$
$$70,560 + 3,528 = 74,088$$
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
